propositions: CLAUDE.md is bloated (665 lines) and duplicates large parts of docs/, degrading instruction-following since it loads every session.
Agent guidance is Claude-locked and unreadable by other harnesses (Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Zed, etc.) that read the cross-vendor AGENTS.md standard.
Migrate to AGENTS.md as the canonical root guidance file, with a thin CLAUDE.md bridging via a one-line @AGENTS.md import and reducing duplicated bulk to docs pointers.
Push portable rebar-usage guidance into docs/ as the single source of truth so audience-specific files point at it rather than duplicating content.
Ship a provider-neutral templates/AGENTS.md for client projects with a drift-guard test keeping a delimited region in sync with authoritative docs.
Provider-config scaffolding (.codex/config.toml, .agents/rules/rebar.md) is untracked/locally excluded, so migration is effectively greenfield and these files must be brought under version control.
